# Overspeed Alerts

> Detect when a tracked device exceeds a configured speed limit for a minimum duration, using a four-level speed limit hierarchy (position > geofence > device > server), and emit a single event at th...

**Category:** Notification · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** gps · tracking · overspeed · speed-limit · alert · fleet · safety

## What this does

Detect when a tracked device exceeds a configured speed limit for a minimum duration, using a four-level speed limit hierarchy (position > geofence > device > server), and emit a single event at th...

Specifies 3 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **device_id** *(hidden, required)* — Device being monitored
- **speed** *(number, required)* — Instantaneous speed from the position record (knots as reported by GPS)
- **speed_limit** *(number, optional)* — Effective speed limit applied to this position (resolved from the hierarchy)
- **geofence_id** *(hidden, optional)* — Zone whose speed limit is being applied when inside a geofence
- **overspeed_state** *(boolean, optional)* — Persisted flag indicating the device is currently in an overspeed episode
- **overspeed_start_time** *(datetime, optional)* — Timestamp when the current overspeed episode began
- **min_duration_seconds** *(number, optional)* — Minimum number of seconds the device must exceed the limit before an event is emitted

## What must be true

- **processing → speed_limit_hierarchy:** Speed limits are resolved in priority order — position-level limit first, then the lowest (or highest, if configured) geofence limit among all active geofences, then device-level limit, then server-wide default
- **processing → geofence_limit_selection:** When multiple geofences each carry a speed limit, the platform selects the most restrictive (lowest) limit by default; this behaviour is configurable
- **processing → speed_multiplier_tolerance:** A speed multiplier (threshold_multiplier > 1.0) may be applied to create a tolerance buffer above the nominal limit before triggering
- **processing → latest_position_only:** Only positions that are the latest for the device are evaluated; outdated positions do not change overspeed state
- **event_emission → single_event_per_episode:** An overspeed event is emitted only when the device transitions from non-overspeeding to overspeeding AND the overspeed condition has persisted for at least min_duration_seconds
- **event_emission → no_repeated_events:** Only one overspeed event is emitted per episode; no repeated events are generated while the device remains over the limit
- **event_emission → state_cleared_on_recovery:** When the device's speed drops below the limit, the overspeed state is cleared; a new episode can begin if speed rises again

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **No Speed Limit Configured** — when no speed limit is configured at any level of the hierarchy, then Speed is not evaluated; no overspeed events are generated.
- **Overspeed Cleared** — when device speed drops below speed_limit; overspeed_state eq true, then Overspeed state cleared; the device can trigger a new event if it speeds again.
- **Overspeed Event Emitted** — when device speed exceeds (speed_limit × threshold_multiplier); overspeed condition has persisted for >= min_duration_seconds; Device was not already in an overspeed episode, then Overspeed event stored; notification handlers alert subscribed users.

## Errors it can return

- `OVERSPEED_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND` — The device referenced does not exist

## Connects to

- **gps-position-ingestion** *(required)* — Position speed values drive overspeed detection
- **geofence-management** *(recommended)* — Geofence-level speed limits override device-level limits
- **gps-device-registration** *(required)* — Speed limits are configured as device attributes
